docs.title: Description
docs.text:
We have defined a metadata schema for describing research software in a structured and interoperable way within the ConnOSS project. The schema represents metadata elements relevant to research software, including software identity, source code, distribution, contributors, development status, technical requirements, and publication-related information.

The schema reuses existing terms primarily from CodeMeta and schema.org, complemented by 12 additional properties derived from a crosswalk analysis of 21 metadata schemas, vocabularies, and ontologies.

The ConnOSS schema is centered around a unified Software type, which integrates properties from schema.org types such as SoftwareSourceCode, SoftwareApplication, CreativeWork and Thing. These types capture different aspects of research software, including its implementation, execution environment, and distribution. While many properties are shared across these representations, they reflect different conceptual perspectives on software, such as its development state, deployment context, and usage requirements. This distinction enables a more comprehensive and flexible description of research software.

The schema will be further complemented by a metadata profile defining obligation levels (mandatory, recommended, optional), cardinality constraints, and usage guidance. This allows consistent and context-aware metadata descriptions while supporting diverse use cases across domains.

All types and properties of the ConnOSS metadata schema are provided through the Data Discovery Engine (DDE) platform, where they are represented in JSON-LD.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: The schema is organized into a core set of 68 properties drawn from schema.org and CodeMeta, plus an extension set of 12 ConnOSS properties.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
ConnOSS Types

ConnOSS types extend the schema.org and CodeMeta vocabularies. All types and properties are also available at the ConnOSS DDE namespace.

TypeDescription SoftwareExtension to schema.org and CodeMeta to describe software source code, software applications, and software releases.

docs.title: Software type
docs.text:
(parent type) schema:SoftwareApplication , schema:SoftwareSourceCode - (type) connoss:Software

Extension to schema.org and CodeMeta to describe software source code, software applications, and software releases.

This type includes properties from schema.org types: Thing, CreativeWork, SoftwareApplication and SoftwareSourceCode, plus the properties below.

PropertyExpected TypeDescription fair4ml#ethicalSocialConsiderations schema:Text A documented concern, requirement, or consideration related to the software's design, deployment, or impact across ethical and social dimensions. Enables transparent disclosure of constraints and mitigation strategies. fair4ml#LegalConsiderations schema:Text A documented concern, requirement, or consideration related to the software's design, deployment, or impact across legal and regulatory dimensions (e.g. licensing constraints, data protection, compliance requirements). Enables transparent disclosure of legal constraints and mitigation strategies. connoss:acknowledgements schema:Text or schema:URL Text or URL containing the people, organizations and other contributors acknowledged by the authors. buildInstructions schema:URL Link to the installation instructions/documentation. schema:contactPoint schema:ContactPoint A contact point for a person or organization. continuousIntegration schema:URL Link to the continuous integration service. developmentStatus schema:Text Description of development status, e.g. Active, inactive, suspended. See repostatus.org. connoss:documentation schema:CreativeWork Resources that describe the software's installation, usage, configuration, development, and deployment intended to support users and developers in understanding and applying the software. embargoEndDate schema:Date Software may be embargoed from public access until a specified date (e.g. pending publication, 1 year from publication). funding schema:Text Funding source (e.g. specific grant). hasSourceCode schema:SoftwareSourceCode Link that states where the software code is for a given software. For example a software registry may indicate that one of its software entries hasSourceCode in a GitHub repository. connoss:implementsSpecification schema:CreativeWork A specification that a software implements, including a standard, API or legally defined level of conformance. e.g. the HTTP standard, the OpenAPI spec, OAuth2. connoss:input bioschemas:FormalParameter A formal specification of the data, files, or parameters that the software accepts as input, including format, type, and whether the input is required. fair4ml#intendedUse schema:Text A concise summary of the primary objective or intended use case for the software. Distinct from general description; this focuses on 'why' the software exists and what problems it solves. isSourceCodeOf schema:SoftwareApplication Link that states where software application is built from a given source code. This is the reverse property of 'hasSourceCode'. issueTracker schema:URL Link to software bug reporting or issue tracking system. connoss:output bioschemas:FormalParameter A formal specification of the data, files, or results that the software produces, including format and type. readme schema:URL Link to software Readme file. referencePublication schema:ScholarlyArticle An academic publication related to the software. schema:relatedLink schema:URL A link related to this object, e.g. related web pages. relatedSoftware schema:SoftwareSourceCode or schema:SoftwareApplication A link to other software that is related by functionality, scientific purpose, or ecosystem context (e.g. alternative implementations, comparable tools, or complementary software). connoss:researchDomain schema:DefinedTerm The discipline, area, or research domain to which this software aligns or belongs to. connoss:softwareContainer schema:CreativeWork or schema:URL A container image or packaged runtime environment of the software. connoss:softwareInterface schema:DefinedTerm The interaction interfaces through which users or other software systems can access, execute, or integrate with software (e.g., CLI, GUI, WebUI, Notebook, API, Library). connoss:testingAction connoss:TestingAction Links the software to a testing activity describing how the software is validated, including the test type, required inputs, test instructions, and produced test results.
